metadata.title:
May DECLARE


BENSON UNFAIR

Darhsrs Say He Keeps His shop Open
Afssr the Hours Provided in His


ID Agreement for Closing.

pages.raw_text:
+ a special meeting of the BARRIERS
union will be held tonight at Central
Labor hall 10 consider the affair Of
THE Benson shop, located on Post
street. Benson has for some time
been trying to have his men con-
sent to work until o'clock In the
evening. which IL one hour over the


time scheduled in the agreement


which he signed with the union. He
also wants his employees to work until


midnight Saturdays. This they have


refused to do and Benson. not to be
outdone, keeps his shop open until


those hours and works himself which


is also considered violation of the
agreement.


Benson argues that this Is being
done by two large non-union and one
open shop OF the city, which Is detri-
mental to the interests OF the other
shops. This he claims forces him to do
|, do what the union says Is a viola ton
Isof his agreement. II is proposed at the
l.meeting tonight to settle the question


and the outcome may declare Ben


sons shop unfair to organized labor.
